title: Development of Innovative Seismic Isolation Techniques for Bridges.
author: Parimala H A
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Bridges are critical infrastructure components, and their seismic resilience is paramount.  Traditional fixed-base bridge designs are vulnerable to seismic forces, potentially leading to catastrophic failures during earthquakes. This research investigates the development and evaluation of innovative seismic isolation techniques for bridges, aiming to enhance their performance under seismic loading. The study explores novel isolator designs and materials, including [mention a specific material or design if known, e.g., fiber-reinforced elastomeric bearings or friction pendulum systems], and assesses their effectiveness through experimental testing and numerical simulations. The performance of the proposed isolation systems is compared with conventional designs, considering factors such as displacement demands, base shear reduction, and energy dissipation capacity. The results demonstrate the potential of these innovative techniques to significantly improve bridge seismic resilience and minimize damage during strong earthquakes.
